title: erica umbellata subsp. major
intro: Erica umbellata subsp. major, commonly known as the bell heather, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the heath family, Ericaceae. It is a low-growing, evergreen shrub that has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Erica umbellata subsp. major include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
